rules:

Choose a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card attached to a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent's) and discard it.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Each player shuffles all cards in his or her discard pile into his or her deck (except for Lysandre's Trump Card).

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Put 1 Pokémon into your hand. (Discard all cards attached to that Pokémon.)

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

This card can only be attached to Psychic Pokémon. This card provides Psychic Energy only while this card is attached to a Psychic Pokémon.

The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is ColorlessColorless less.

(If this card is attached to anything other than a Psychic Pokémon, discard this card.)
Double Colorless Energy provides ColorlessColorless Energy.

Choose a Pokémon Tool or Special Energy card attached to a Pokémon in play (yours or your opponent's) and discard it.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack of the Pokémon this card is attached to, you may ignore all effects of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. (You may only use effects that let you flip coins again, including effects from other cards, once during your turn.)

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Draw 3 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

fullDisplayName: Target Whistle Team Flare Gear 106/119 Phantom Forces
rules:
Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent's discard pile onto his or her Bench.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Each Metal Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) can't be affected by any Special Conditions. (Remove any Special Conditions affecting those Pokémon.)

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 5 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Flip a coin. If heads, draw 3 cards.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

fullDisplayName: Robo Substitute Team Flare Gear 102/119 Phantom Forces
rules:
Play this card as if it were a 30 HP Colorless Basic Pokémon. At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may discard this card from play.

This card can't retreat. If this card is Knocked Out, your opponent can't take any Prize Cards for it.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Your turn does not end if the Pokémon this card is attached to becomes M Manectric-EX.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Each player shuffles all cards in his or her discard pile into his or her deck (except for Lysandre's Trump Card).

You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

cardName: Jamming Net Team Flare Hyper Gear
fullDisplayName: Jamming Net Team Flare Hyper Gear 98/119 Phantom Forces
rules:
Attach this Pokemon Tool to 1 of your opponent's Pokemon-EX that doesn't already have a Pokemon Tool attached to it.

The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 less damage to all Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

When this card is removed from a Pokémon for any reason, put this card in its owner's discard pile.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

attacks.name: Smithereen Smash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: With their powerful ears, they can heft boulders of a ton or more with ease. They can be a big help at construction sites.
They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through.

attacks.name: Daunt
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

attacks.name: Boomburst
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its howls can be heard over six miles away. It emits all sorts of noises from the ports on its body.

attacks.name: Energy Press
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It shouts loudly by inhaling air, and then using its well-developed stomach muscles to exhale.

attacks.name: Screaming Fit
attacks.effect: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.

flavorText: It usually murmurs but starts crying loudly if it senses danger. It stops when its ear covers are shut.

attacks.name: Psybite
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Psychic Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: While it sleeps, the head of its tail keeps watch. The tail doesn't need to sleep.

attacks.name: Tender Vengeance
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Charge Dash
attacks.effect: You may do 20 more damage. If you do, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: The eggs it lays are filled with happiness. Eating even one bite will bring a smile to anyone.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lucky Punch
attacks.effect: If you don't have exactly 7 cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It is said to deliver happiness. Being compassionate, it shares its egg with injured people.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land or rest.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Very protective of its territory, it flaps its short wings busily to dart around at high speed.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your deck.

flavorText: This very friendly Dragon-type Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.

attacks.name: Gooey
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can't see anything.

attacks.name: Gooey
attacks.effect: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon

flavorText: The weakest Dragon-type Pokémon, it lives in damp, shady places, so its body doesn't dry out.

attacks.name: Crazy Headbutt
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Their heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The two heads do not get along. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader.

attacks.name: Gnaw Off
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

attacks.name: Sparkle
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Diamond Storm
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from each of your Fairy Pokémon.

flavorText: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

attacks.name: Light Pulse
attacks.effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

attacks.name: Lick Away
attacks.effect: Remove all Special Conditions from this Pokémon.

flavorText: To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and as sticky as cotton candy.

attacks.name: Lead
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Bright Garden
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Grass Pokémon and Fairy Pokémon you have in play.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Dull Light
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them.

attacks.name: Slash Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Slash Blast
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Poke Through
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Spiral Rush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

attacks.name: Steel Drop
attacks.effect: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Steam Blast
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It dwells in volcanic caves. It digs in with its cross-shaped feet to crawl on ceilings and walls.

attacks.name: Chrono Wind
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Full Metal Impact
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

flavorText: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.
Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor's back contained a mysterious power.

attacks.name: Iron Wing
attacks.effect: Discard a Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: After nesting in bramble bushes, the wings of its chicks grow hard from scratches by thorns.

attacks.name: MAXamar
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Gentle Bite
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 60 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Their beautiful form comes from the muscles they have developed. They run silently in the night.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its cute act is a ruse. When victims let down their guard, they find their items taken. It attacks with sharp claws.

attacks.name: B Cancel
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A Pokémon that was formed by 108 spirits. It is bound to a fissure in an odd keystone.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Nightmare Mambo
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: If one utters a deep cry, many Murkrow gather quickly. For this, it is called "Summoner of Night."

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It hides any shiny object it finds in a secret location. Murkrow and Meowth loot one another's stashes.

attacks.name: Overdrive Smash
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The solar rays it absorbs are processed in its energy core and fired as a ball of light.

attacks.name: Core Heal
attacks.effect: Discard a Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon and heal 50 damage from it.

flavorText: Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals.
Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

attacks.name: Submission Hold
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't attach Energy from his or her hand to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

attacks.name: Rock Cannon
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

attacks.name: Horror Note
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.

flavorText: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

attacks.name: Night March
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Pokémon in your discard pile that have the Night March attack.

flavorText: The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.effect: Put 6 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: Being consumed in Chandelure's flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.effect: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Night March
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Pokémon in your discard pile that have the Night March attack.

flavorText: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

attacks.name: Trip Over
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life force of any who follow it.

attacks.name: Dream of Memories
attacks.effect: Shuffle 3 cards from your discard pile into your deck.

attacks.name: Dream Dance
attacks.effect: Both Active Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: The mist emanating from their foreheads is packed with the dreams of people and Pokémon.

attacks.name: See Through
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

attacks.name: Double Headbutt
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears before people and Pokémon who are having nightmares and eats those dreams.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Swallow Up
attacks.effect: If, before doing damage, your opponent's Active Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It swallows anything whole. It sweats toxic fluids from its follicles to douse foes.

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It has a small heart and brain. Its stomach compromises most of its body, with enzymes to dissolve anything.

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to build up its counterstrike.

attacks.name: Phantom Gate
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It flies so silently through the dark on its four wings that it may not be noticed even when nearby.

attacks.name: Swoop Across
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Once it bites, it will not stop draining energy from the victim even if it gets too heavy to fly.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Even though it has no eyes, it can sense obstacles using ultrasonic waves it emits from it mouth.

attacks.name: Parabolic Spark
attacks.effect: Discard as many Lightning Energy attached to your Pokémon as you like. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.

flavorText: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: They make their home in deserts. They can generate their energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement.

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: They make their home in deserts. They can generate their energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement.

attacks.name: Cobweb Trip
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with your opponent's Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Electroweb
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They employ an electrically charged web to trap their prey. While it is immobilized by shock, they leisurely consume it.

attacks.name: Night March
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Pokémon in your discard pile that have the Night March attack.

flavorText: Since it can't generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.

attacks.name: Trick Sticker
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon's Weakness is now Lightning until the end of your next turn. (The amount of Weakness doesn't change.)

attacks.name: Pachi
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

attacks.name: Turbo Bolt
attacks.effect: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.

attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 60 more damage.

rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Super Deep Dive
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore.

attacks.name: Meddling
attacks.effect: Attach 3 Energy cards from your opponent's discard pile to his or her Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Ensnaring Spray
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is mostly seawater. It's said there's a castle of ships Jellicent have sunk on the seafloor.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: They paralyze prey with poison, then drag them down to their lairs, five miles below the surface.

attacks.name: Neon Sign
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It lives on the deep-sea floor. It attracts prey by flashing the patterns on its four tail fins.
The line running down its side can store sunlight. It shines vividly at night.

attacks.name: Hyper Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Second Strike
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It usually moves slowly, but it goes at blinding speed when it attacks and bites prey.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

attacks.name: Fury Strikes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is small but rough and tough. It won't hesitate to take a bite out of anything that moves.

attacks.name: Guard Claw
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.
If it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.

attacks.name: Inferno Onrush
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The stronger the opponent it faces, the more heat surges from its mane and the more power flows through its body.

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Jet Shoot
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is increased by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: In the fever of an exciting battle, it showers embers from the gaps between its feathers and takes to the air.
From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass.
These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

attacks.name: Coordinate
attacks.effect: Choose up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon that have no Pokémon Tools attached to them. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card, and attach it to that Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Leaf Storm
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from each of your Grass Pokémon.

flavorText: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves by using the sticky silk secreted from its mouth.

attacks.name: Protect
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

attacks.name: Windfall
attacks.effect: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Surprise Strike
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It prefers to battle by biting apart foes' heads instantly while flying by at high speed.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won't miss prey—even those behind it.

attacks.name: Dizzying Wind
attacks.effect: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card from his or her hand during his or her next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that card has no effect. (Your opponent still discards that card.)

attacks.name: Noxious Scales
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: The scales it scatters will paralyze anyone who touches them, making that person unable to stand.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its big eyes are actually clusters of tiny eyes. At night, its kind is drawn by light.
